comment: Cross-language FFI conformance KAT. v1 (read-only path: open/recovery/read_block) frozen by docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-15-ffi-b6-conformance-kat-design.md (PR #58). v2 (lifecycle path: save/share/trash/restore) added by docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-17-ffi-b6-v2-lifecycle-conformance-kat-design.md (issue #59); v2 was extended in place (no version bump) by docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-08-purge-empty-trash-399-design.md (issue #399, Task 11a) to add purge_block / empty_trash / restore-of-purged vectors continuing the same open_writable_happy lifecycle chain. Generated by `cargo test --release --workspace -- --ignored generate_conformance_kat --nocapture`; intentional protocol changes regenerate, diffs are human-reviewed. The #399 purge vectors below were hand-authored, NOT regenerated (the generator only re-emits read_block_happy.expected.records and save_block_insert_happy.expected.post_state.read_block.records). Verified by core/tests/conformance_kat.rs::replay_conformance_kat (every cargo test) plus the Swift + Kotlin host runners in ffi/secretary-ffi-uniffi/tests/{swift,kotlin}/ (Task 11b, pending as of Task 11a: the runners do not yet handle purge_block / empty_trash and will fail on these vectors until updated).

vectors.name: open_password_happy
vectors.description: open_vault_with_password against golden_vault_001 using the pinned password from golden_vault_001_inputs.json:password.

vectors.name: open_password_wrong
vectors.description: open_vault_with_password with a literal wrong password → typed VaultError.WrongPasswordOrCorrupt.

vectors.name: open_password_nonexistent_folder
vectors.description: open_vault_with_password against a bogus folder path → typed VaultError.FolderInvalid.

vectors.name: open_recovery_happy
vectors.description: open_vault_with_recovery using the pinned 24-word phrase from golden_vault_001_inputs.json.

vectors.name: open_recovery_wrong_phrase
vectors.description: open_vault_with_recovery using vault_002's phrase against vault_001's folder → typed VaultError.WrongMnemonicOrCorrupt.

vectors.name: open_recovery_short_phrase
vectors.description: open_vault_with_recovery with a 3-word phrase → typed VaultError.InvalidMnemonic with detail containing 'got 3'. The substring matches the core error: 'expected 24 words, got 3'.

vectors.name: read_block_happy
vectors.description: read_block on golden_vault_001's sole block (the 'Personal logins' block). Record list populated by the generator; see core/tests/data/golden_vault_001_inputs.json for the source-of-truth plaintext.

vectors.name: read_block_unknown_uuid
vectors.description: read_block with a UUID not present in the manifest → typed VaultError.BlockNotFound.

vectors.name: read_block_oversize_uuid
vectors.description: read_block with a 17-byte block_uuid (one byte too many) → synthesized InvalidArgument at the replay layer; uniffi binding rejects with VaultError.InvalidArgument on Swift + Kotlin.

vectors.name: read_block_wrong_length_uuid
vectors.description: read_block with a non-16-byte block_uuid → synthesized InvalidArgument at the replay layer (FfiVaultError doesn't expose this variant; it lives on the uniffi-projected VaultError only). Each replay engine emits its host's analogue.

vectors.name: read_block_zero_length_uuid
vectors.description: read_block with an empty block_uuid (0 bytes) → synthesized InvalidArgument at the replay layer; uniffi binding rejects with VaultError.InvalidArgument on Swift + Kotlin.

vectors.name: open_writable_happy
vectors.description: v2 head of the writable chain. Copies golden_vault_001/ to a tempdir, opens the copy with the pinned password. Cached output is mutated by chained write vectors; tempdir is held by the replay engine for the full run.

vectors.name: save_block_insert_happy
vectors.description: Inserts block 0xAB*16 with one note record into the writable vault. Asserts block_count goes 1→2, find_block returns the new uuid, and read_block(new_uuid) round-trips the input record (records placeholder filled by the generator).

vectors.name: save_block_invalid_input
vectors.description: save_block with a 1-byte device_uuid (must be 16). Synthesized InvalidArgument at the Rust replay layer; real InvalidArgument at the uniffi namespace layer on Swift+Kotlin. Does not mutate cache.

vectors.name: share_block_happy
vectors.description: Shares the new block (saved by the previous vector) with alice (user_uuid from golden_vault_001/contacts/). Asserts recipient_count goes 1→2.

vectors.name: share_block_recipient_already_present
vectors.description: Re-shares the same block with alice (the recipient just added). The replay engine includes alice's card in existing_recipient_uuid_hexes so existing_recipient_cards covers every recipient currently on the block.

vectors.name: trash_block_happy
vectors.description: Trashes the new block. Asserts block_count goes 2→1.

vectors.name: trash_block_unknown_uuid
vectors.description: Trashes uuid 0x00*16 which was never live in the manifest. Bridge returns BlockNotFound.

vectors.name: restore_block_happy
vectors.description: Restores the just-trashed block. Asserts block_count goes 1→2 and find_block returns the restored uuid again.

vectors.name: restore_block_not_in_trash
vectors.description: Restores a uuid (0x00*16) that has never been live and never been trashed. Bridge returns BlockNotInTrash. Re-restoring the just-restored block would return BlockUuidAlreadyLive instead (live-collision path), so a never-touched uuid is used here to exercise the not-in-trash path.

vectors.name: restore_block_already_live
vectors.description: Re-restores the same uuid that was just restored by restore_block_happy. The block is currently live in the manifest, so the bridge returns BlockUuidAlreadyLive (live-collision path). Chains after restore_block_not_in_trash (error vector — state unchanged from restore_block_happy: block_count=2, abababab... live) to keep the lifecycle chain linear.

vectors.name: save_block_insert_purge_target
vectors.description: #399 Task 11a. Inserts a fresh owner-only block (0xEE*16) into the writable vault so it can be trashed then purged in isolation from the abababab... block (which carries a non-owner recipient from share_block_happy).

fields.name: note
fields.value_utf8: owner-only, headed for the trash then a purge

vectors.name: trash_block_purge_target
vectors.description: #399 Task 11a. Trashes the owner-only block just inserted, so purge_block has a live TrashEntry to act on.

vectors.name: purge_block_owner_only_happy
vectors.description: #399 Task 11a. Permanently purges the owner-only trashed block. Asserts the PurgeReport classifies was_shared=false with recipient_count=1 (owner only) and removes at least one on-disk trash/ file. block_count is unaffected — purge only marks the TrashEntry purged, it never touches manifest.blocks.

vectors.name: restore_block_of_purged_block
vectors.description: #399 Task 11a. Attempts to restore the block just purged above. A purge is permanent, so restore must be rejected with the dedicated BlockPurged variant (Task 8) rather than falling back to BlockNotInTrash or an integrity failure.

vectors.name: save_block_insert_empty_trash_a
vectors.description: #399 Task 11a. First of two blocks seeded for the empty_trash aggregate scenario: owner-only (never shared).

vectors.name: trash_block_empty_trash_a
vectors.description: #399 Task 11a. Trashes the owner-only block above, leaving it as a not-yet-purged TrashEntry for empty_trash to target. The already-purged 0xEE*16 entry from purge_block_owner_only_happy remains in manifest.trash but must NOT be re-targeted (purged_at_ms is already set).

vectors.name: save_block_insert_empty_trash_b
vectors.description: #399 Task 11a. Second block seeded for the empty_trash aggregate scenario: will be shared to alice below before being trashed, so empty_trash's aggregate has one shared + one owner-only entry.

fields.name: note
fields.value_utf8: shared with alice, headed for empty_trash

vectors.name: share_block_empty_trash_b
vectors.description: #399 Task 11a. Shares the fresh 0xFB*16 block with alice (same fixture contact card as share_block_happy) so it classifies as shared once trashed.

vectors.name: trash_block_empty_trash_b
vectors.description: #399 Task 11a. Trashes the now-shared 0xFB*16 block, completing the seeded trash: 0xEE (already purged, excluded), 0xFA (owner-only), 0xFB (shared).

vectors.name: empty_trash_seeded_happy
vectors.description: #399 Task 11a. Purges the entire seeded trash in one batch. Targets are 0xFA (owner-only) and 0xFB (shared) only — the already-purged 0xEE entry is excluded by empty_trash's not-yet-purged filter. Asserts the aggregate EmptyTrashReport: purged_count=2, shared_count=1, owner_only_count=1, unknown_count=0, files_removed>=2, files_failed=0.

vectors.name: restore_block_of_empty_trashed_a
vectors.description: #399 Task 11a. Attempts to restore the 0xFA block that empty_trash_seeded_happy just purged. Same BlockPurged assertion as restore_block_of_purged_block, exercised via the batch (empty_trash) path instead of the single (purge_block) path.

vectors.name: save_block_invalid_record_uuid
vectors.description: save_block with a 1-byte record_uuid (must be 16). Rust dispatch synthesizes InvalidArgument at the test layer (the bridge crate's RecordInput is type-bounded to [u8; 16] so wrong length never reaches it); Swift + Kotlin pass the raw bytes through and uniffi's namespace-layer convert_record_input surfaces real VaultError.InvalidArgument. Symmetric with save_block_invalid_input (which exercises the device_uuid length-check path). Chains after open_writable_happy; pre-mutation error means downstream-chain state is unaffected.

fields.name: title
fields.value_utf8: irrelevant — error fires before save_block is invoked

vectors.name: open_device_secret_happy
vectors.description: open_with_device_secret against golden_vault_001 using the pinned device slot uuid + secret from golden_vault_001_inputs.json. Replays through bridge::device::open_with_device_secret; same observable open output as open_password_happy (display_name Owner, single block).

vectors.name: open_device_secret_wrong_secret
vectors.description: open_with_device_secret with the correct slot uuid but a wrong 32-byte device secret (all 0xff) → typed VaultError.WrongDeviceSecretOrCorrupt (the device_kek AEAD unwrap fails).

vectors.name: open_device_secret_absent_slot
vectors.description: open_with_device_secret with a device uuid that has no devices/<uuid>.wrap file → typed VaultError.DeviceSlotNotFound. device_secret is the pinned correct secret (irrelevant: the missing-slot error fires before any unwrap).

vectors.name: open_device_secret_short_secret
vectors.description: open_with_device_secret with a 31-byte device secret (must be 32). Rust dispatch synthesizes InvalidArgument at the test layer (the bridge crate's open_with_device_secret signature is type-bounded to &[u8; 32] so wrong length never reaches it); Swift + Kotlin pass the raw bytes through and uniffi's namespace-layer length check surfaces real VaultError.InvalidArgument. Symmetric with read_block_wrong_length_uuid / save_block_invalid_input.
